entities.name: SCLEROSPORA GRAMINICOLA
entities.description: The causal agent of pearl millet downy mildew, primarily heterothallic with two mating types G1 and G2, but can also be homothallic.

entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: The host crop affected by downy mildew caused by Sclerospora graminicola.

entities.name: DOWNY MILDEW
entities.description: The disease caused by the pathogen Sclerospora graminicola in pearl millet.

entities.name: HETEROTHALLISM
entities.description: A sexual compatibility type requiring two distinct mating types for reproduction, characterized by self-sterility.

entities.name: HOMOTHALLISM
entities.description: A sexual compatibility type allowing for self-fertility within a single isolate.

entities.name: MATING TYPE G1
entities.description: One of the two distinct sexual compatibility mating types in Sclerospora graminicola.

entities.name: MATING TYPE G2
entities.description: One of the two distinct sexual compatibility mating types in Sclerospora graminicola.

entities.name: F1 PROGENIES
entities.description: The first generation offspring resulting from a cross between two self-sterile isolates of S. graminicola.

entities.name: CROSSING
entities.description: The process of mating isolates to establish progenies and determine sexual compatibility types.

entities.name: OOSPORES
entities.description: Sexual spores produced by G1G2 progenies when crossed with parents.

entities.name: MENDELIAN SEGREGATION
entities.description: The pattern of inheritance observed for the mating type locus in S. graminicola.

entities.name: MATING TYPE LOCUS
entities.description: The genetic location that determines the sexual compatibility type of the pathogen.

entities.name: SEXUAL REPRODUCTION
entities.description: The process of genetic recombination that plays a role in the evolution of new genetic recombinants in the pathogen.

entities.name: GENETIC STRUCTURE
entities.description: The overall genetic composition of Sclerospora graminicola populations.

entities.name: VIRULENCE
entities.description: The ability of the pathogen to cause disease, with potential for evolution of new types.
